hypothesis: If, for a given atomic configuration evaluated by a direct-force–pretrained neural network interatomic potential, the Force Delta U∆ (RMS disagreement between −∇Ē and the predicted non-conservative forces) increases, then the true force error relative to DFT increases and is better predicted than by within-family ensemble variance because internal disagreement between conservative and non-conservative force heads quantifies epistemic uncertainty even on adversarially perturbed out-of-distribution structures.
